title: The headline activity is cancelled
note: The simplest shape. One slot goes empty and one option is closed. A warm up.

title: The local cab is repriced mid trip
note: Transport goes over its allocation. There is a cheap fix inside the category if the agent looks.

title: Train fares surge, there is no cheap way into the city
note: The forced tradeoff. Even the cheapest arrival plus the cheapest local transport costs more than the transport allocation, so no amount of rebooking alone can fix it. The agent has to reallocate from another category first, then rebook.

title: The evening plan and its only fallback both die
note: A cascade with no clean answer. Day 1 Evening has nothing left to book, so the agent has to accept a gap, say so honestly, and put the freed money somewhere useful.

title: The only replacement busts its category and nobody can lend
note: The forced refusal. Day 2 Morning empties and the only option left for it is the Rs 3,200 balloon, which is Rs 700 more than activity has. The hotel spike leaves stay with Rs 100 spare and transport has Rs 450, so the whole trip can lend Rs 550 against a Rs 700 gap and reallocate_budget is refused whichever donor is asked. The cheap dorm that would have paid for it is sold out too. Nothing works until the agent spends less somewhere first, for example dropping the private cab to the auto pass, which frees real allocation, then reallocating, then rebooking.

title: A cancelled tour and a hotel price spike at once
note: Two independent breaks in one go. The agent has to notice both, not stop after the first.
